mnemonics: SET & RESET are LATCHed in place (like a door latch) until a new command changes them.
NOR Latch: SET is 0-Active, RESET is 0-Active. Remember 'NOR' starts with 'N' for 'Negative' logic.
keyPoints: A latch is a level-sensitive memory element; its state depends on the enable input level.
The most basic latch is the SR (Set-Reset) latch, built from cross-coupled NOR or NAND gates.
Latches are transparent; when enabled (E=1), the output Q directly follows the data input D.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that setting both S and R to 1 (on a NOR-based SR latch) creates an invalid/forbidden state.
Assuming a gated D latch is edge-triggered; it captures data on the entire high level of the clock, not just the edge.
